title: Monthly Farm Tour
description: Get a behind-the-scenes look at Coastal Roots Farm. On this guided tour, you’ll enjoy a gentle walk through our ever-evolving gardens, educational spaces, chicken coops, compost operations, and vibrant Food Forest. Learn about our unique growing methods and how we cultivate food, community, and justice. The tour includes a hill and mixed terrain. We recommend wearing flat shoes, a sun hat, and bringing a water bottle. Learn about accessibility accommodations on the Farm. Pre-registration required. 🗓️ Sunday, April 12 🕒 1-2:30 p.m. | 📍Meet at the Coastal Roots Farm Coffee Cart 🎟️ $25/person. Register About Coastal Roots Farm We're nonprofit community farm and education center rooted in Jewish values and regenerative agriculture. We grow over 80,000 pounds of organic produce each year on our 18-acre farm, with more than 75% donated to community members experiencing food insecurity. Each year, we also welcome over 15,000 youth and adults for outdoor education, Jewish life programs, and community events that inspire environmental stewardship, climate action, and connection to Jewish tradition.
excerpt: Get a behind-the-scenes glimpse of Coastal Roots Farm’s production methods as you enjoy a gentle walk through the ever-evolving and diverse vegetable, herb, and flower gardens.

title: Farm-to-Table Dinner – with Menu by Lana – SOLD OUT
description: Celebrate a Season of Gratitude at Our April Farm-to-Table Dinner Join us for a special evening honoring Javier Guerrero’s nearly eight years of leadership at Coastal Roots Farm. Under Javier’s guidance, the Farm has grown into a thriving, mission-driven community—expanding access to fresh food, deepening educational impact, and cultivating connection through land and tradition. This gathering offers a meaningful opportunity to celebrate his legacy and the community he helped build. Set in the heart of our 18-acre regenerative farm, this intimate dinner will feature a seasonally inspired, multi-course pescatarian menu crafted by Lana , a Solana Beach restaurant known for its California coastal cuisine. Guests will enjoy organic vegetables harvested from the Farm, sustainably sourced fish, and wine from Propaganda Wine Co. and beer pairings—shared in community under the open sky. As we reflect on all that has been cultivated over the past eight years, we also look ahead. Proceeds from this evening support Coastal Roots Farm’s work to nourish our community—providing fresh food to those in need, educating thousands of youth, and ensuring this land continues to serve as a place of connection, resilience, and growth. Seats are limited to 75 guests for this intimate gathering in our Coastal Kitchen. Reserve Your Spot at the Table 📅 April 12, 2026 | 🕕 5 - 8 PM 💰Tickets: General: $150/person Farm Friend: $500/person $100 of your ticket is tax-deductible. Join the Waitlist Dine with Purppose Your presence—and your generosity—helps carry this work forward. One of the most meaningful ways to honor Javier’s leadership is to invest in the future of the Farm and the communities it serves. For those who are able, Farm Friend tickets provide additional philanthropic support , helping sustain the programs, land, and people at the heart of this work. Guests will also have the opportunity to make an additional gift in honor of Javier , supporting the continued growth of the Farm and the community he has helped cultivate. Part of Our Farm-to-Table Dinner Series This dinner is part of our seasonal Farm-to-Table Dinner Series , celebrating local flavors, regenerative agriculture, and the power of food to connect us. title: Gratitude Happy Hour
description: We invite the community to join us as we gather in gratitude and celebration of Javier’s leadership at a Happy Hour Gratitude Celebration. Join us to toast to Javier's eight years at Coastal Roots Farm and continue this work together, rooted in the same care, purpose, and connection that Javier helped cultivate. 🗓️ Wednesday, April 15 ⏰ 4-6 p.m. 🎟️ $36/person Register Here The event will take place on the Farm's barn lawn. Refreshments will be provided.

title: From Soil to Soul: Adult Farm Camp
description:
Why should kids have all the fun? Join us for a Farm camp just for grown-ups ! Immerse yourself in regenerative agriculture, farm-fresh food, nature-inspired art, earth-based Judaism, climate education, and the invitation to make new friends. The day will contain opportunities to put your hands in the dirt, make art, harvest and cook, and find joy in embodied Jewish wisdom. 🗓️ Sunday, April 19 ⏰ 9:30 am - 3:30 pm. 🎟️ $75/person Pricing Includes all materials, lunch, drinks, and snacks. Limited scholarship available – we want you here. Don’t let the cost keep you from attending. Register Today! What the Day Looks Like… 9:30-9:45 a.m. Check-in, morning coffee/tea, shmoozing 9:45-10:15 a.m . Shalom Circle: Connect with each other, set an intention, singing, and schedule for the day 10:15-11:45 a.m. Morning session to include 3 choices: something physical, something spiritual, and something artistic 11:45 a.m.-12 p.m. Who is with you on this journey? Connecting more with each other 12-1 p.m. Lunch and shmooze, visit Farm Stand 1:15-2:45 p.m. Afternoon session to include 3 choices: something physical, something spiritual, and something artistic 3-3:30 p.m. Closing Shalom Circle: reflections, singing, and closing ritual Morning Option 1: Rabbi Mike Rothbaum: Jewish Food Ethics Option 2: Cooking with Kim O'Donnell-Boyd Option 3: Hands in the Soil with Farm Educators Afternoon Option 1: Qi-Gong Psalms with Susan Freeman Option 2: Visual Art with Suzanne Bates Option 3: Hands in the Soil with Farm Educators Facilitators include: Sharone Oren, Education Manager Rebecca Joy Fletcher, Director of Jewish Life Age: Anyone over the age of 18 who is excited to be part of camp! Register Today! FAQs What’s the schedule for the day?

What the Day Looks Like...

9:30-9:45 a.m. Check-in, morning coffee/tea, shmoozing

9:45-10:15 a.m. Shalom Circle: Connect with each other, set an intention, singing, and schedule for the day

10:15-11:45 a.m. Morning session to include 3 choices: something physical, something spiritual, and something artistic

11:45 a.m.-12 p.m. Who is with you on this journey? Connecting more with each other

12-1 p.m. Lunch and shmooze, visit Farm Stand

1:15-2:45 p.m. Afternoon session to include 3 choices: something physical, something spiritual, and something artistic

3-3:30 p.m. Closing Shalom Circle: reflections, singing, and closing ritual

I want to do all the sessions! Will some of the workshops repeat so that I can experience them all?

Unfortunately, we don’t have enough time to do all the sessions multiple times. If you’re having a hard time deciding which session to attend, please let us know!

Is the event accessible?
  • At the Farm we strive to welcome folks of all levels of ability. Please refer to our Accessibility page for more information.
  • If you have specific concerns, reach out to Sharone@coastalrootsfarm.org
What should I bring?
  • Comfortable clothes and shoes, reusable water bottle and hat.
  • Be prepared to dress in layers, as the weather does fluctuate a bit on the Farm. If you’re intending on getting your hands in the soil, bring clothes you don’t mind getting dirty.
Will there be childcare?

From Soil to Soul is a camp just for the grown-ups! No childcare will be provided. The programming is best for guests 18 and older.

Do I need prior Jewish knowledge or experience?

Nope! Open to all.

Is the food kosher/vegetarian/vegan-friendly?

We will be MAKING vegetarian food and snacks. A questionnaire asking about dietary needs will be sent prior to the start of camp.

What happens if it rains?

We will be closely monitoring the weather. If there’s light rain, the program will go on. In case of heavy rain, thunderstorms or lightning, we will cancel and inform you of next steps.

Cancellations policy

Cancellation > 2 weeks in advance: Eligible for 90% refund (upon request).
Cancellation ≤ 2 weeks in advance: Not eligible for refund.
Cancellation post event: Not eligible for refund.

title: Yoga on the Farm
description: Outdoor Yoga on the Farm Stretch, breathe, and reconnect—surrounded by the beauty of the farm. Join us for a midweek reset where movement meets nature: soft grass under your feet, fresh air in your lungs, and the rhythms of the land guiding your flow. Whether you’re new to yoga or a seasoned practitioner, this welcoming outdoor class invites you to slow down, tune in, and cultivate a sense of balance—both on and off the mat. 🗓️ Every Wednesday, April 22 - June 3 ⏰ 4-5 p.m. 🎟️ $10 per class Sign Up Know before you go: Dress in layers in case it's very warm or begins to get chilly If you forget your mat, no worries! We will have plenty for you to choose from Don't forget your sunscreen and reusable water bottle. Optional: Bring blocks or a towel Instructor: Julie Snyder Block has over 25 years of yoga practice and holds a 200-hour RYT yoga teacher certification. She loves teaching a Vinyasa-style yoga that blends breath, movement, and mindfulness. When she’s not on the mat, Julie enjoys spending time with family and friends, practicing newborn and pediatric medicine, playing pickleball, and exploring crafts. Her classes are a reflection of her passion for both wellness and connection.

title: Eagle Presentation
description: Join us at Eagle Mesa for a presentation about our bald & golden eagles! Learn about these two non-releasable raptors, like what they eat, why they reside at the Living Coast, fun facts, and more! *All events subject to cancellation at the discretion of our staff.

title: Shark & Ray Presentation
description: Visit the Shark & Ray Experience to learn about the diverse range of species that inhabit it, including leopard sharks, bat rays, diamond rays, shovelnose guitarfish, horn sharks, and more! This exhibit features a touch tank where you can interact with some of these residents while learning about them. *All presentations are subject to cancellation at the discretion of the staff.

title: Sea Turtle Presentation
description: Join us at Turtle Lagoon to learn about green sea turtles, even the ones you can find right here in San Diego Bay! A Living Coast educator will tell you all about sea turtles, like their diet, shell, fun facts, and more! *All animal presentations are subject to cancellation at the discretion of our staff.

title: Sweetwater Safari Hike
description: Join us every Saturday as we explore the Sweetwater Marsh Trail on the San Diego Bay National Wildlife Refuge! We will learn about the history of the Sweetwater Marsh, the salt marsh ecosystem, and the plants and wildlife thriving in this protected space. Backpacks with fun activities, including magnifying glasses and binoculars, will be available for families to use while on the hike. Check-in time: 11:30 am Duration: 30-45 Minutes Meeting Location: We will meet outside the front doors of the Living Coast Discovery Center located at 1000 Gunpowder Point Drive, Chula Vista, CA 91910. Driving directions: From the I5 freeway, exit at E street and head west to the Living Coast Discovery Center parking lot. title: Bat Hike at Sweetwater River Trail
description: J oin us for a beautiful sunset hike to discover the different bat species that call the San Diego National Wildlife Refuge home! Compost bins are available for $35 to Chula Vista residents who have attended one of the City’s composting workshops or the Master Composter Course. Chula Vista residents can receive free admission to the Living Coast on select Sundays! Click here to sign up.

title: Vermicomposting Workshop
description: Offered the last Sunday of every month at 11:30 AM. Vermicomposting uses worms to convert waste into fertilizer for your garden! This course will teach you how you can get started with your own worm bin and how to care for your worm friends! This class is included with admission. Chula Vista residents can receive free admission to the Living Coast on select Sundays! Click here to sign up.

organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
title: Slow Down Swap Party at the Rancho Bernardo Earth Day Fair
description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego for a FREE clothing swap at the Rancho Bernardo Community Council's 10th Annual Earth Day Fair! Let’s slow down our consumption, slow down for the day, and exchange some clothing and accessories. Join us in reducing our textile waste to celebrate Earth Day alongside amazing vendors, local organizations, and our community! Event date: Sunday, April 12th Location: Webb Park Swap Event time: 10:00 AM – 1:00 PM A Few Reminders: Please register for the event. However, walk-ins are welcome! Donation limit of 1 grocery bag, please ensure they are lightly used- no rips, stains, or odors You don’t need to donate in order to take some home with you, anyone can bring home a new outfit! Closest Bus Stop: West Bernardo Dr & 16882 (Bus Route 945) ~ 28 minute walk Thank you Environmental Services Department of San Diego for funding this event.

organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
title: City of Carlsbad’s Earth Month Celebration
description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego for a day of family-friendly and sustainable activities at the City of Carlsbad’s Earth Month Celebration, "Love Our Planet: There’s No Place Like Earth." We will be having a clothing swap, DIY sustainable cleaning starter kits, as well as a community mural pop-up! Clothing Swap- Bring in your gently-used clothing and “shop” for free items that others have donated—no money exchanged! It’s a great way to promote sustainability, reduce textile waste, and give clothes a second life instead of sending them to the landfill. Everyday Sustainable Cleaning Starter Kits- Make and take a citrus all-purpose cleaner made from citrus peel scraps and learn about how to incorporate affordable and low-waste practices in our everyday lives! Community Mural Pop-up- Grab a brush and take part in our community paint-by-numbers mural! We will be repurposing old banners into an art piece encouraging food waste prevention. Participate in one or all and enjoy the rest of the day in the park as we celebrate our planet! City of Carlsbad Earth Month Celebration Event Date: Saturday, April 18 Event Time: 9 a.m. to 1 p.m. Location: Poinsettia Community Park This annual celebration features interactive stations with hands-on activities, demonstrations and practical advice from city staff and community organizations.

Please check back on other workshop dates or contact info@waterscaperebates.com to learn more about our workshops. Residents of the Unincorporated San Diego County may be eligible to receive a free rain barrel for attending*. Must Register by clicking the blue button below. *Limit one barrel per eligible address; While supplies last; Prior workshop attendees and rebate recipients not eligible for free barrel.

organization: I Love A Clean San Diego

description: Join I Love A Clean San Diego for a free clothing swap during the City of La Mesa’s Earth Day Fair ! Celebrate the beauty and abundance of our planet and stop by our clothing and accessories swap. Enjoy a day at MacArthur Park, slow down the consumption of new items, and exchange your clothing, shoes, and accessories you want to pass on to another home to keep our fabrics out of the landfill. Let’s recirculate our clothing back into our local community and get a great ‘new-to-you’ outfit while we’re at it. Event date: Saturday, April 18 th Location: La Mesa Community Garden at MacArthur Park at 5080 Memorial Dr. Event time: 11:00 am – 3:00 pm A Few Reminders: Please register for the event, however walk-ins are welcome! Donation limit of 1 grocery bag; please ensure they are lightly used – no rips, stains, or odors You don’t need to donate in order to take some home with you; anyone can bring home a new outfit! organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
title: Transform Food Waste with Vermicomposting
description: Learn to reduce your food waste and compost at home! Join I Love A Clean San Diego for our free Transform Food Waste with Vermicomposting Workshop in the Old Poway Park’s Great room on Sunday, April 19 th at 10:30am. During this workshop, we’ll cover: Ways to reduce food waste in the kitchen Discuss the importance of keeping food waste out of landfills Explain how red wigglers transform food waste into fresh compost How to properly utilize the organic waste recycling program with your green bin Tips and tricks on how to use the organic recycling bin Attendees can make and take home a vermicomposting bin (worm composting) during the workshop we provide all supplies and information which will be provided to start composting at home! Supplies are limited to one bin per household, with priority given to residents of the Poway. We’ll also have the Sprouting Station open for attendees to plant fruit and vegetable seeds to take home and grow – use the finished compost from your vermicomposting bin to fertilize! Registration is required. Thank you to the City of Poway for sponsoring this event!

organization: I Love A Clean San Diego
title: Compost and Mulch Giveaway – 5/9 at Snapdragon Stadium
description: Join us for our upcoming Compost and Mulch Giveaway Event! This event is available for City of San Diego residents ONLY! Have you ever wondered what happens after you toss yard trimmings and food scraps into your green bin? The organic waste is collected and brought to the Miramar Greenery (located at the Miramar Landfill) where it is turned into compost and mulch! These products available at no cost to residents of the City of San Diego and can be picked up during normal business hours. To increase opportunities for residents to pick up compost, I Love A Clean San Diego and the City of San Diego are partnering to host Compost and Mulch Giveaways on select weekends. Get your hands on some nutrient-rich compost for your garden at no cost. Whether you're a seasoned gardener or just starting out, this event is perfect for anyone looking to give their plants a boost. Swing by and grab some compost to help your garden thrive! Things to bring: Shovel Gloves Containers Tarp and bungees for truck beds (ALL TRUCK BEDS MUST BE COVERED!) Things to know: Be prepared to self load compost All participants are REQUIRED to sign a release waiver Exact coordinates : (32.78322141834713, -117.1171162524508) Please be aware, in order to collect compost you are REQUIRED to sign a waiver. All participants will need to sign a waiver upon entering the event. About the Miramar Greenery: At the Miramar Greenery, compost is made from yard trimmings and food scraps that have been ground, placed in windrows, turned and watered for 70 days. During this time, microorganisms digest the carbon and nitrogen rich mixture, causing the windrow to sustain temperatures of 140-165 F. This process eliminates most weed seeds and pathogens while breaking down the organic material into beneficial soil nutrients. Finished compost is screened to a particle size of one-half inch or less which also removes film plastic from the final product. For more information, visit https://www.sandiego.gov/environmental-services/miramar Practical Application and Benefits : Compost can be used as an amendment to improve soil texture and increase nutrient and water holding capacity. In San Diego, where soil lacks many essential plant nutrients, mixing compost in with the soil is one of the best things you can do for your yard and garden. Note: This event will occur rain or shine, however if severe weather occurs the days leading up to the event, the event will be rescheduled or canceled. If needed, registrants will receive electronic communication as soon as possible before the event. This event is made possible by the City of San Diego Environmental Services Department and facilitated by I Love A Clean San Diego.

Office Tasks include: Glove pairing and folding Bucket washing Supply organizing and packing Paper shredding And more! A few requirements for this program: Minimum age 16 Have your own transportation to our office Sign liability-release waiver (if you're under 18, provide 1 signed by a guardian) These shifts occur every second Wednesday of the month, so if you're interested, click below to register!

title: Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour
description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us for a Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour! Join us for a guided Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour through the beautiful waterways of South San Diego Bay, ending in the restored Chula Vista Wetlands. This scenic, two-hour paddle offers a unique opportunity to explore coastal habitats while learning about the wildlife and conservation efforts that make South San Diego Bay so special. Along the way, you may spot osprey diving for fish, great blue herons and egrets wading in the shallows, nesting terns (April to September), and a variety of migrating birds (October to March). With a little luck, guests can even catch a glimpse of a Pacific green sea turtle cruising through the bay! This tour is welcoming to all skill levels and moves at a comfortable pace, with plenty of time to enjoy the surroundings. The route is about two hours long, and wind conditions can vary, adding a bit of adventure while still remaining accessible for beginners. What to Expect: Wildlife Viewing: Paddle through the waters, keeping an eye out for local wildlife, from native birds to coastal critters. Educational Insights: Our expert guides will provide fascinating information about the local ecosystems and conservation efforts. Chula Vista Wetlands: Paddle toward the restored wetlands, a vital area for wildlife and an important conservation success story. Come prepared for a fun and challenging adventure while experiencing the beauty of the Chula Vista Wetlands. Sign up for our next Wildlife Kayaking Eco Tour today! Best of all, every tour supports our education programs for South Bay youth! Find more information at www.oceanconnectors.org Group Discounts available on 6 or more tickets. Email eco-tours@oceanconnectors.org

title: Community Kayak Cleanup
description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us as we clean up our wetlands! Help keep our local waters beautiful and thriving by joining a kayak-based cleanup adventure! Paddle through coastal habitats, collect litter, and protect marine life while enjoying the beauty of the bay. No prior experience needed—just bring your enthusiasm, and we’ll provide the equipment and guidance. Every piece of trash you collect helps create a cleaner, healthier environment for our coastal communities. What to Expect: Litter Removal: Paddle through scenic waterways to remove litter that threatens wildlife and pollutes local ecosystems. Guided Experience: Enjoy a safe, eco-friendly adventure led by our experienced team, perfect for all skill levels. Gear Provided: We supply everything you need, including kayaks, paddles, life jackets, gloves, bags, and grabbers. Kayak and Safety Instructions: Before setting out, we’ll provide a brief orientation on paddling techniques and safety guidelines to ensure a fun and secure experience. Litter Sorting and Data Collection: Help us sort and document the litter collected, contributing valuable data to conservation efforts. Expect to Get Dirty: Be ready for a hands-on adventure! You might get wet, muddy, or a little dirty while making a big impact. Join us in making a difference on the water—register now for our next kayak cleanup!

title: Tidepool Exploration
description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us on a tide pooling adventure! Step into a world of fascinating marine life on our guided Tide Pool Exploration Eco Tour! Perfect for all ages, this adventure brings you up close with the unique and diverse creatures of our local tide pools. With our knowledgeable guides, you’ll learn about tide pool ecosystems and the vital role they play in coastal habitats. What to Expect: Hands-On Exploration: Carefully observe tide pool life, from sea stars, crabs, and anemones to some of the more elusive residents that reward a careful eye, like two-spot octopus, Spanish shawl nudibranchs, spiny lobsters, and occasionally even eels! Eco-Education: Learn from our guides about tide pool ecosystems, conservation, and how to protect these delicate habitats. Family-Friendly Adventure: Designed for all ages, this tour is perfect for families, students, and anyone curious about marine life. Dive into the wonders of the tide pools—sign up for a memorable, up-close look at marine biodiversity! Best of all, every tour supports our education programs for South Bay youth! Find out more at www.oceanconnectors.org Group Discounts available on 6 or more tickets. Email eco-tours@oceanconnectors.org

title: Birding & Brews – Happy Hour Edition
description: Wind down your afternoon with birds, brews, and bayside views! Join us for a relaxed, guided birdwatching walk along the beautiful Bayshore Bikeway at South San Diego Bay. Then, wrap up the day with a complimentary craft beverage at Novo Brewing—perfect for happy hour with a conservation twist. What to Expect: Birdwatching Walk: Stroll the Bayshore Bikeway with expert guides to spot native and migratory birds in their natural habitat. Ecology & Conservation: Discover fascinating facts about the South Bay’s wildlife and ongoing efforts to protect our coastal ecosystems. Happy Hour Cheers: Finish your adventure with a refreshing drink at Novo Brewing, with non-alcoholic options available too—a great way to toast to community and conservation. Every Birding & Brews tour helps fund our education programs for South Bay youth—so you can enjoy your happy hour knowing you’re making a difference!

title: Community Restoration Day
description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us in restoring San Diego's vital wetland habitats! Get hands-on experience in restoring coastal sagescrub habitats by helping remove invasive species, planting native plants, and supporting local wildlife. Each event wraps up with a guided nature walk where you can spot native birds and learn more about the area’s natural ecosystem. What to Expect: Invasive Species Removal : Help clear invasive plants that threaten local biodiversity. Litter Removal : Help keep our local environment clean and healthy by removing trash and debris. Native Planting : Restore balance by reintroducing native species that support local wildlife. Nature Walk : Enjoy a guided exploration of the habitat, perfect for birding and learning more about the ecosystem you’ve helped restore. Site Maintenance : Site Maintenance: Lend a hand in keeping our restoration site thriving. Tasks may include digging, planting, watering, and general upkeep to ensure the health and beauty of our native plants. All ages and experience levels are welcome. Bring your friends, family, and community members to help make a lasting impact—together, we can restore these vital habitats and protect our coast for generations to come.

title: North County Nature Paddle Eco Tour
description: Be an Ocean Connector and join us on one of our North County Kayaking adventures! Discover the natural beauty of Agua Hedionda Lagoon on a guided kayaking tour through this unique coastal ecosystem in Carlsbad. As you paddle through calm waters near the lagoon’s wetlands, keep an eye out for snowy egrets, great egrets, and great blue herons foraging along the shoreline. You may also spot a belted kingfisher darting across the water, killdeer along the muddy upland edges, or red-tailed hawks perched high on the powerlines above. Along the way, learn about local conservation efforts, wetland restoration, and the rich biodiversity that makes this lagoon such an important habitat. This eco tour is suitable for all experience levels and offers a meaningful way to connect with nature while supporting environmental education and stewardship. What to Expect: Wildlife Viewing: Watch for native birds and other wildlife as you explore the lagoon by kayak. Educational Insights: Learn about lagoon ecology, restoration projects, and sustainable aquaculture. Birding Support: Binoculars and bird identification guides are provided to enhance your experience. Guided Exploration: Enjoy expert-led interpretation while paddling through this peaceful marine habitat. Join us for an inspiring on-the-water experience highlighting the importance of preserving San Diego’s coastal resources. Group Discounts available on 6 or more tickets. Email eco-tours@oceanconnectors.org
